---
title: "Econometría I"
subtitle: "Grado en Economía and Grado en ADE — Universidad de Granada"
---

::: {style="text-align: right; margin-bottom: 1em;"}
[← Back to Teaching](../teaching.html){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-secondary}
:::

A second-year undergraduate course that walks students from the *idea* of an econometric model to the practical estimation, inference, and diagnostic skills required to analyse real economic data. Taught in English with R.

## Open textbook

An open companion textbook — ***Econometrics I: Theory, Practice, and Applications*** — is freely available under a **CC BY 4.0** license. It consolidates lecture notes, R tutorials, and interactive self-assessments into a single reproducible volume, built with Quarto.

📖 Read the **[English edition](https://agaggero.github.io/econometrics-i/)** · **[edición en español](https://agaggero.github.io/econometria-i/)**.

## Chapter template

Each topic follows the same six-section structure:

1. **Learning outcomes** — explicit objectives
2. **Motivating empirical question** — a real-world question the topic answers
3. **Theory** — concepts, derivations, key results
4. **Worked example in R** — full code walked through step by step
5. **Self-check** — interactive quiz
6. **Exercises** — graded ★ easy · ★★ medium · ★★★ analytical

## Topics

1. **[Initial Concepts](https://agaggero.github.io/econometrics-i/chapters/01-initial-concepts.html).** What econometrics is; correlation vs causation; randomisation and quasi-experimental designs; cross-section, time series, panel and pooled data; basic exploratory analysis in R.

2. **[The Simple Linear Regression Model](https://agaggero.github.io/econometrics-i/chapters/02-simple-regression.html).** OLS derivation; Gauss–Markov assumptions; unbiasedness; the BLUE property; $R^2$; `lm()` in R.

3. **[The Multiple Linear Regression Model](https://agaggero.github.io/econometrics-i/chapters/03-multiple-regression.html).** Matrix form; *ceteris paribus* interpretation; MLR.1–MLR.5; omitted-variable bias; adjusted $R^2$.

4. **[Inference](https://agaggero.github.io/econometrics-i/chapters/04-inference.html).** Classical Linear Model assumptions; sampling distributions of $\hat{\beta}_j$; $t$-tests; confidence intervals; $F$-tests for joint significance; $p$-values.

5. **[Prediction and Model Selection](https://agaggero.github.io/econometrics-i/chapters/05-prediction.html).** Point and interval prediction; in-sample vs out-of-sample performance; model-selection criteria.

6. **[Nonlinear Relationships and Qualitative Information](https://agaggero.github.io/econometrics-i/chapters/06-nonlinear-qualitative.html).** Logarithms, polynomials, and interactions; dummy variables and dummy–continuous interactions.

7. **[Heteroskedasticity](https://agaggero.github.io/econometrics-i/chapters/07-heteroskedasticity.html).** Detection (Breusch–Pagan, White); consequences for inference; heteroskedasticity-robust standard errors; weighted least squares.

8. **[Autocorrelation](https://agaggero.github.io/econometrics-i/chapters/08-autocorrelation.html).** Time-series complications; Durbin–Watson; Newey–West standard errors.

**Appendices** — [mathematical and statistical prerequisites](https://agaggero.github.io/econometrics-i/chapters/appendix-a-prerequisites.html) · [Wooldridge ↔ Carter–Griffiths–Lim crosswalk](https://agaggero.github.io/econometrics-i/chapters/appendix-b-wooldridge-cgl-crosswalk.html) · [statistical tables](https://agaggero.github.io/econometrics-i/chapters/appendix-c-tables.html).

## Course logistics

- **Programme.** Grado en Economía · Grado en Administración y Dirección de Empresas
- **Level.** Second-year undergraduate
- **Language of instruction.** English
- **Software.** R via RStudio
- **Reference textbook.** Wooldridge, *Introductory Econometrics: A Modern Approach* (latest edition), cross-referenced with Carter, Griffiths, Lim, *Principles of Econometrics*, 5th edition.
- **Assessment.** Continuous assessment (practicals and quizzes) plus a final exam.
